title: AI Review Engine Optimization

summary.overview: This PR introduces an inference caching layer for the AI review engine, reducing redundant model invocations for previously analyzed code patterns. Benchmarks show a 40% reduction in average response latency and a 35% decrease in GPU compute costs. A few concerns around cache invalidation strategy and memory management should be addressed before production deployment.

riskAssessment.summary: The caching layer introduces new state management concerns. Stale cache entries could lead to outdated review results, and unbounded cache growth may cause memory pressure under high load.
items: Cache invalidation relies solely on TTL without content-based invalidation when models are updated
No maximum memory limit configured for the in-process cache
Cache key generation does not account for model version changes
Performance under cache stampede conditions has not been tested

strengths: Well-structured cache abstraction that cleanly separates caching concerns from inference logic
Semantic cache key generation using code embeddings provides intelligent deduplication of similar inputs
Comprehensive benchmark suite included with before/after metrics for latency and throughput
LRU eviction policy is correctly implemented with configurable capacity thresholds
Good use of read-through caching pattern that keeps the consuming code simple and unaware of cache internals

issues.message: Cache invalidation is based solely on a 24-hour TTL. When the underlying model is updated or fine-tuned, stale cached results will continue to be served until the TTL expires, potentially returning outdated review assessments.
issues.recommendation: Implement a model-version-aware invalidation strategy that clears or tags cache entries when the inference model version changes, using a versioned cache key prefix.

issues.message: The in-process LRU cache has no configured memory ceiling. Under sustained high-throughput conditions with diverse inputs, cache growth could consume excessive heap memory and trigger garbage collection pauses.
issues.recommendation: Configure a maximum memory budget for the cache (e.g., 512MB) and add memory-pressure monitoring that triggers early eviction when the threshold is approached.

issues.message: Cache hit and miss events are logged at DEBUG level, making it difficult to monitor cache effectiveness in production without changing log levels.
issues.recommendation: Emit cache hit/miss metrics via the application metrics pipeline (e.g., Prometheus counters) rather than relying on log-level configuration for observability.

recommendations: Add model version to the cache key to automatically invalidate entries when the inference model is updated
Configure a memory ceiling for the cache and add heap usage monitoring with alerting thresholds
Implement cache stampede protection using a request coalescing pattern for concurrent identical inference requests
Add a cache warming strategy for commonly reviewed code patterns during service startup to reduce cold-start latency
